\\":\\\"#5b403e\\\",\\\"primary\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"primary_container\\\":\\\"#ff5351\\\",\\\"primary_fixed\\\":\\\"#ffdad7\\\",\\\"primary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"secondary\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"secondary_container\\\":\\\"#822625\\\",\\\"secondary_fixed\\\":\\\"#ffdad7\\\",\\\"secondary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"surface\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"surface_bright\\\":\\\"#3a3939\\\",\\\"surface_container\\\":\\\"#201f1f\\\",\\\"surface_container_high\\\":\\\"#2a2a2a\\\",\\\"surface_container_highest\\\":\\\"#353534\\\",\\\"surface_container_low\\\":\\\"#1c1b1b\\\",\\\"surface_container_lowest\\\":\\\"#0e0e0e\\\",\\\"surface_dim\\\":\\\"#131313\\\",\\\"surface_tint\\\":\\\"#ffb3ae\\\",\\\"surface_variant\\\":\\\"#353534\\\",\\\"tertiary\\\":\\\"#71d5e0\\\",\\\"tertiary_container\\\":\\\"#319ea9\\\",\\\"tertiary_fixed\\\":\\\"#8ef2fd\\\",\\\"tertiary_fixed_dim\\\":\\\"#71d5e0\\\"},\\\"designMd\\\":\\\"# Design System Strategy: The Cinematic Curator\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Overview \u0026 Creative North Star\\\\nThe North Star for this design system is **\\\\\\\"The Cinematic Curator.\\\\\\\"** \\\\n\\\\nIn a world of cluttered OTT interfaces, this system rejects the \\\\\\\"infinite grid\\\\\\\" in favor of a high-end editorial experience. We treat digital real estate like a luxury film program. To move beyond a \\\\\\\"template\\\\\\\" look, we leverage **intentional asymmetry**—such as oversized hero titles overlapping content cards—and a rigorous **tonal layering** strategy. This creates a sense of \\\\\\\"physical\\\\\\\" depth where content isn't just displayed; it is presented. The interface should feel like a darkened theater where only the most important elements catch the light.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 2. Colors \u0026 Surface Architecture\\\\nOur palette is rooted in the \\\\\\\"Absolute Dark\\\\\\\" philosophy. We do not use pure black (#000) for surfaces to avoid \\\\\\\"crushing\\\\\\\" the visual depth. Instead, we use deep charcoals and warm bordeaux-tinted grays.\\\\n\\\\n#### The \\\\\\\"No-Line\\\\\\\" Rule\\\\n**Explicit Instruction:** Designers are prohibited from using 1px solid borders to define sections or containers. \\\\nBoundaries must be defined solely through:\\\\n1.  **Background Shifts:** Moving from &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B).\\\\n2.  **Vertical Breathing Room:** Utilizing the &#96;8&#96; (2.75rem) or &#96;10&#96; (3.5rem) spacing tokens to signal a change in context.\\\\n\\\\n#### Surface Hierarchy \u0026 Nesting\\\\nTreat the UI as a series of stacked layers. The closer an element is to the user, the \\\\\\\"brighter\\\\\\\" its surface becomes:\\\\n*   **Base Layer:** &#96;surface&#96; (#131313) for the main application background.\\\\n*   **Sectional Layer:** &#96;surface_container_low&#96; (#1C1B1B) for large content groupings.\\\\n*   **Interactive Layer:** &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534) for cards, modals, or hovering states.\\\\n\\\\n#### Glass \u0026 Gradient Implementation\\\\nTo move beyond a flat \\\\\\\"flat\\\\\\\" look, use **Glassmorphism** for floating navigation bars or player controls.\\\\n*   **The Recipe:** Use &#96;surface_container&#96; at 70% opacity with a &#96;backdrop-filter: blur(20px)&#96;.\\\\n*   **Signature Gradients:** For primary CTAs, use a subtle linear gradient (Top-Left to Bottom-Right) from &#96;primary&#96; (#FFB3AE) to &#96;primary_container&#96; (#FF5351) to add a \\\\\\\"pulse\\\\\\\" of energy to the Coral-Red accent.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 3. Typography\\\\nThe system uses a sophisticated pairing of **Manrope** (Display/Headlines) and **Inter** (Body/Labels).\\\\n\\\\n*   **Display (Manrope):** These are our \\\\\\\"Editorial Statements.\\\\\\\" Use &#96;display-lg&#96; (3.5rem) for movie titles in hero sections. Use negative letter-spacing (-0.02em) to create a high-fashion, dense look.\\\\n*   **Headline \u0026 Title (Manrope/Inter):** &#96;headline-md&#96; (1.75rem) serves as the primary gateway into a category. It should feel authoritative.\\\\n*   **Body \u0026 Label (Inter):** &#96;body-md&#96; (0.875rem) is used for synopses and metadata. Inter’s high x-height ensures legibility against the dark &#96;surface&#96; backdrop.\\\\n*   **The Tonal Hierarchy:** Primary information uses &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1). Secondary metadata (Year, Rating, Genre) must use &#96;on_surface_variant&#96; (#E4BDBA) to reduce visual noise.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 4. Elevation \u0026 Depth\\\\nWe eschew traditional drop shadows for **Tonal Layering**.\\\\n\\\\n*   **The Layering Principle:** Place a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; (#0E0E0E) card inside a &#96;surface_container_high&#96; (#2A2A2A) section. This \\\\\\\"recessed\\\\\\\" look creates an elegant, carved-out feel without the need for a single pixel of stroke.\\\\n*   **Ambient Shadows:** If an element must float (e.g., a Context Menu), use a shadow with a 40px blur and 6% opacity, using the &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; color as the shadow tint. This mimics natural light dissipation.\\\\n*   **The Ghost Border:** If a boundary is required for accessibility, use the &#96;outline_variant&#96; token at **15% opacity**. This provides a \\\\\\\"hint\\\\\\\" of a container without breaking the minimal aesthetic.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 5. Components\\\\n\\\\n#### Buttons\\\\n*   **Primary:** Background: &#96;primary&#96; gradient. Shape: &#96;0.5rem&#96; (8px). Typography: &#96;title-sm&#96; (Inter Semi-Bold).\\\\n*   **Secondary:** Background: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534). For a premium feel, add a 10% opacity &#96;primary&#96; tint to the background.\\\\n*   **Ghost/Tertiary:** No background. Use &#96;on_surface&#96; text with a &#96;primary&#96; icon.\\\\n\\\\n#### Content Cards (The Core Unit)\\\\n*   **Geometry:** Always use &#96;lg&#96; (1rem/16px) for the outer container and &#96;md&#96; (0.75rem/12px) for internal elements (like \\\\\\\"New\\\\\\\" badges).\\\\n*   **Nesting:** Place &#96;on_surface&#96; typography directly onto the image using a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; gradient overlay at the bottom 30% of the card.\\\\n*   **Spacing:** Content within cards uses &#96;3&#96; (1rem) padding.\\\\n\\\\n#### Chips \u0026 Badges\\\\n*   **Selection Chips:** Use &#96;secondary_container&#96; (#822625) for the active state to provide a moody, deep-red glow that isn't as aggressive as the primary CTA.\\\\n\\\\n#### Lists \u0026 Carousels\\\\n*   **Strict Rule:** **Forbid dividers.** Use &#96;6&#96; (2rem) of horizontal whitespace between carousel items. If list items require separation, use a subtle background shift to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; on hover.\\\\n\\\\n#### Navigation Rail (Unique OTT Component)\\\\n*   Instead of a top bar, use a side-aligned Navigation Rail. Use **Glassmorphism** (70% opacity + blur) to allow movie poster art to bleed through as the user scrolls, keeping the experience immersive.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### 6. Do’s and Don'ts\\\\n\\\\n*   **DO** use &#96;surface_bright&#96; (#3A3939) for hover states on dark cards to create a \\\\\\\"lit from within\\\\\\\" effect.\\\\n*   **DO** use intentional asymmetry—allow a \\\\\\\"Featured\\\\\\\" movie poster to take up 65% of the screen width while metadata takes up 35%.\\\\n*   **DON'T** use 100% white (#FFFFFF) for long-form text; use &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1) to reduce eye strain in dark environments.\\\\n*   **DON'T** ever use a solid &#96;outline&#96; at 100% opacity. It breaks the \\\\\\\"Cinematic\\\\\\\" immersion and looks like a generic web app.\\\\n*   **DO** prioritize \\\\\\\"The Breath.\\\\\\\" If a screen feels crowded, double the spacing token (e.g., move from &#96;5&#96; to &#96;10&#96;). High-end design is defined by what you leave out.\\\",\\\"colorVariant\\\":\\\"FIDELITY\\\",\\\"overridePrimaryColor\\\":\\\"#E5383B\\\",\\\"overrideNeutralColor\\\":\\\"#0F0F0F\\\",\\\"spacingScale\\\":3},\\\"screenInstances\\\":[{\\\"id\\\":\\\"051e00c95b48445a8f09b36be4a0cf01\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/051e00c95b48445a8f09b36be4a0cf01\\\",\\\"x\\\":2465,\\\"y\\\":10,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1302},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"0622da3a85f04ec5ab26c7a156b5f38d\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/0622da3a85f04ec5ab26c7a156b5f38d\\\",\\\"x\\\":1478,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":2152,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"14dc681cf67d4ac99b836bb1977ee992\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/14dc681cf67d4ac99b836bb1977ee992\\\",\\\"x\\\":2002,\\\"y\\\":8,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1525},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"531dbe2167a049c3ab0cb003bb697909\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/531dbe2167a049c3ab0cb003bb697909\\\",\\\"x\\\":2075,\\\"y\\\":2447,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":884,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"71cd040f2d2947c79e5abbc1a2534b6f\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/71cd040f2d2947c79e5abbc1a2534b6f\\\",\\\"x\\\":1622,\\\"y\\\":2409,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1302,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"7219607cf63041f18d10a5294e572910\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/7219607cf63041f18d10a5294e572910\\\",\\\"x\\\":4079,\\\"y\\\":51,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1076,\\\"hidden\\\":true},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"8c9dd9c64d66497dab5106dc5ed4be05\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/8c9dd9c64d66497dab5106dc5ed4be05\\\",\\\"x\\\":3426,\\\"y\\\":19,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":1096},{\\\"id\\\":\\\"98752323786c449585eeb94cf5b80b3b\\\",\\\"sourceScreen\\\":\\\"projects/18386357471434296476/screens/98752323786c449585eeb94cf5b80b3b\\\",\\\"x\\\":6082,\\\"width\\\":390,\\\"height\\\":884, Typography\\nThe system uses a sophisticated pairing of **Manrope** (Display/Headlines) and **Inter** (Body/Labels).\\n\\n*   **Display (Manrope):** These are our \\\"Editorial Statements.\\\" Use &#96;display-lg&#96; (3.5rem) for movie titles in hero sections. Use negative letter-spacing (-0.02em) to create a high-fashion, dense look.\\n*   **Headline \u0026 Title (Manrope/Inter):** &#96;headline-md&#96; (1.75rem) serves as the primary gateway into a category. Shape: &#96;0.5rem&#96; (8px). Typography: &#96;title-sm&#96; (Inter Semi-Bold).\\n*   **Secondary:** Background: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; (#353534). For a premium feel, add a 10% opacity &#96;primary&#96; tint to the background.\\n*   **Ghost/Tertiary:** No background. Use &#96;on_surface&#96; text with a &#96;primary&#96; icon.\\n\\n#### Content Cards (The Core Unit)\\n*   **Geometry:** Always use &#96;lg&#96; (1rem/16px) for the outer container and &#96;md&#96; (0.75rem/12px) for internal elements (like \\\"New\\\" badges).\\n*   **Nesting:** Place &#96;on_surface&#96; typography directly onto the image using a &#96;surface_container_lowest&#96; gradient overlay at the bottom 30% of the card.\\n*   **Spacing:** Content within cards uses &#96;3&#96; (1rem) padding.\\n\\n#### Chips \u0026 Badges\\n*   **Selection Chips:** Use &#96;secondary_container&#96; (#822625) for the active state to provide a moody, deep-red glow that isn't as aggressive as the primary CTA.\\n\\n#### Lists \u0026 Carousels\\n*   **Strict Rule:** **Forbid dividers.** Use &#96;6&#96; (2rem) of horizontal whitespace between carousel items. If list items require separation, use a subtle background shift to &#96;surface_container_low&#96; on hover.\\n\\n#### Navigation Rail (Unique OTT Component)\\n*   Instead of a top bar, use a side-aligned Navigation Rail. Use **Glassmorphism** (70% opacity + blur) to allow movie poster art to bleed through as the user scrolls, keeping the experience immersive.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 6. Do’s and Don'ts\\n\\n*   **DO** use &#96;surface_bright&#96; (#3A3939) for hover states on dark cards to create a \\\"lit from within\\\" effect.\\n*   **DO** use intentional asymmetry—allow a \\\"Featured\\\" movie poster to take up 65% of the screen width while metadata takes up 35%.\\n*   **DON'T** use 100% white (#FFFFFF) for long-form text; use &#96;on_surface&#96; (#E5E2E1) to reduce eye strain in dark environments.\\n*   **DON'T** ever use a solid &#96;outline&#96; at 100% opacity. It breaks the \\\"Cinematic\\\" immersion and looks like a generic web app.\\n*   **DO** prioritize \\\"The Breath.\\\" If a screen feels crowded, double the spacing token (e.g., move from &#96;5&#96; to &#96;10&#96;). High-end design is defined by what you leave **Checking UI consistency**

I'm adjusting the max card width calculation to account for fixed 160px cards with 32px gaps, using `Math.min(160, Math.max(120, windowWidth - 64))`. 

**Adjusting card width calculation**

I'm refining the card width calculation to use `Math.min(160, windowWidth - spacing['2xl'] * 2)` for better responsiveness. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what changed after comparing your home flow to Stitch **“Home (Updated Nav)”** (`projects/18386357471434296476/screens/f8df68ac4f5b4dbaa5bfa92c7d62f756`) and its HTML.

### Design alignment (vs your old UI)

| Area | Stitch | App updates |
|------|--------|-------------|
| **Brand** | `#E5383B` logo + wordmark, `movie` icon, notifications `#E4BDBA` | `colors.brand_accent`, `HomeHeader` uses movie + Manrope wordmark, notifications muted |
| **Top bar** | Fixed `h-20` (80px), horizontal `px-8` (32) | `HOME_HEADER_CONTENT_HEIGHT = 80`, header/list gutters use `spacing['2xl']` (32) |
| **Genre chips** | Active: `secondary-container` + `on-surface`; inactive: `surface-container-low` + `on-surface-variant` + faint border | Active uses `secondary_container` + `on_surface`; inactive uses `surface_container_low` + border `chip_inactive_border` |
| **Hero** | 450px band, image ~60% opacity, bottom scrim, large display title, **solid** primary CTA (`on-primary` text), glassy Details | `HERO_IMAGE_HEIGHT = 450`, dimmed image, gradient scrim, **solid** `primary` / `on_primary` Watch, Details with frosted `surface_container_highest` + hairline border |
| **Rows** | `text-2xl` titles, muted “See All”, `gap-8`, 160× poster tiles | `headline_row` (24px), See All = `on_surface_variant`, 32px gaps, card width capped at **160** (shrinks if needed) |
| **Loading** | Spinner + small caps label | `ActivityIndicator` + uppercase label (Stitch-style tracking) |
| **Tabs** | Active `#E5383B` | `tabBarActiveTintColor` → `brand_accent` |

### Files touched

- `src/theme/colors.ts` — `brand_accent`, `on_primary`, `on_primary_container`, `chip_inactive_border`
- `src/theme/typography.ts` — `headline_row` for row titles
- `src/components/home/homeLayout.ts` — header height 80
- `src/components/home/HomeHeader.tsx` — Stitch header layout/colors/icons
- `src/components/home/GenreChipStrip.tsx` — chip states + padding/gutters
- `src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx` — full hero rebuild to match Stitch
- `src/components/home/MovieRow.tsx` — spacing, typography, card width, gaps
- `src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx` — poster surface + title/subtitle scale
- `src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx` — 32px gutters, genre/hero vertical rhythm
- `src/components/home/HomeLoadingFooter.tsx` — spinner + label (replaces skeleton strip)
- `src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx` — active tab color

Tests: `npm test` — all 4 suites passed.

**Note:** Stitch’s mock also labels a second carousel as **“Action”** while the app still uses **Trending / Top Rated / Discover** with real TMDB data. Layout and styling now follow the Stitch home; renaming rows to fixed genres would be a product/API change, not just styling.

---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Here’s what was aligned with Stitch **Home (Updated Nav)** typography (`font-headline` = Manrope, `font-body` = Inter) and the HTML text scale.

### `src/theme/typography.ts`
New tokens so home UI matches the design system in one place:

| Token | Stitch reference |
|--------|------------------|
| `home_wordmark` | Header: Manrope extrabold ~`text-2xl`, tight tracking |
| `home_hero_title` | Hero title: `text-5xl` Manrope extrabold, `-0.03em` tracking |
| `home_hero_synopsis` | Hero copy: `text-base` (16px) Inter |
| `home_hero_badge` | Badge: `10px` caps, wide letter-spacing |
| `home_hero_cta` | Watch / Details: bold-style Inter ~16px |
| `home_genre_chip` | Chips: `text-sm` semibold, slight `tracking-wide` |
| `home_row_title` | Row titles: `text-2xl` Manrope bold |
| `home_row_see_all` | “See All”: `text-sm` semibold |
| `home_card_title` / `home_card_meta` | Card title `text-sm` semibold, meta `text-xs` |
| `home_load_more` | Footer label: `text-xs` uppercase, wide tracking |
| `home_tab_label` | Tab labels: `10px` uppercase (Inter-SemiBold; app has no Inter Medium file) |

### `src/theme/colors.ts`
- `brand_accent` (`#E5383B`) — wordmark / active tab  
- `on_primary_container` (`#5C0008`) — text on coral badge  

### Components wired to those tokens
- **`HomeHeader.tsx`** — `home_wordmark` + `brand_accent`, `movie` + `notifications` Material icons, muted notifications  
- **`GenreChipStrip.tsx`** — `home_genre_chip`; inactive label color → `on_surface_variant` (Stitch inactive pills)  
- **`HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`** — badge / title / synopsis / CTA labels use the hero tokens; badge text → `on_primary_container`  
- **`MovieRow.tsx`** — `home_row_title`, `home_row_see_all` + `on_surface_variant` for “See All”  
- **`ContentCard.tsx`** — `home_card_title` + `home_card_meta` (used on home rows; same card typography elsewhere)  
- **`HomeLoadingFooter.tsx`** — `home_load_more`  
- **`RootNavigator.tsx`** — tab label `home_tab_label`, active tint `brand_accent`  

### `HomeScreen.tsx`
No copy changes were required for parity; it still only shows error strings with existing `typography.body_md` (Inter 14), which is fine for empty/error states.

Tests: `npm test` — all passing.

**Note:** Tab labels use **Inter-SemiBold** at 10px because only **Inter-Regular** and **Inter-SemiBold** are bundled; Stitch uses **Inter Medium** at 10px — visually very close.

---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Here’s what was updated to match Stitch project **18386357471434296476** (sizes, radii, Material-style icons).

### Layout & card sizes (`src/theme/spacing.ts`)
- Added **`stitchLayout`**: `screenGutter` 32 (`px-8`), **`posterWidth` 160**, **`posterHeight` 240**, **`posterRowGap` 32 (`gap-8`)**, **`heroBandHeight` 450 (`h-[450px]`)**.
- **`MovieRow`** always uses **160×240** cards with **`layoutVariant="stitch"`** on `ContentCard`, list padding **`screenGutter`**, separators **`posterRowGap`**.
- **`HomeScreen`** hero width: `SCREEN_WIDTH - 2 * screenGutter`.
- **`HomeHeader`**: `minHeight` 80 (`h-20`), horizontal **`screenGutter`**.
- **`GenreChipStrip`**: horizontal **`screenGutter`**, chip vertical padding **10** (`py-2.5`).
- **Skeletons** (`HomeScreenSkeleton`, `DiscoverRowSkeleton`, `SkeletonPosterCard`) use the same gutters, gaps, hero height, and poster width.

### Corner radii (`radius` in `src/theme/spacing.ts`)
Aligned with the Stitch Tailwind extension (**4 / 8 / 12 px**):
- **`stitchXs` (4px)** — small chips (e.g. rating badge, hero “New release” badge).
- **`stitchLg` (8px)** — **`rounded-lg`**: hero Watch / Details buttons, skeleton header pills, **tab bar top** (`rounded-t-lg`).
- **`stitchXl` (12px)** — **`rounded-xl`**: poster frames, hero shell, skeleton hero block.
- Kept legacy **`sm` (12)** and **`md` (16)** for watchlist / other screens.

### `ContentCard` (`src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx`)
- **`layoutVariant="stitch"`**: fixed **240** poster height + **`stitchXl`** corners + **`surface_container_low`**.
- Default variant: still **2:3** aspect ratio, same **12px** corners and surface token for consistency.

### Hero (`src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`)
- Fixed **450px** height, **`stitchXl`** outer radius, image **~60% opacity** (via `imageStyle`), badge **`stitchXs`**, CTAs **`stitchLg`**.
- **`play-arrow`** remains **MaterialIcons** (same glyph family as Stitch’s filled play).

### Tab bar icons (`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`)
- Switched from **Ionicons** to **`react-native-vector-icons/MaterialIcons`** to mirror **Material Symbols** in the HTML:
  - **Home**: `home-filled` (focused) / `home` (inactive)
  - **Search**: `search`
  - **Watchlist**: `bookmark` / `bookmark-border`
  - **Profile**: `person` / `person-outline`
- **Header** was already **MaterialIcons** (`movie`, `notifications`).

### Colors (`src/theme/colors.ts`)
- **`chip_inactive_border`** for inactive genre outline (Stitch `border-outline-variant/10`).

### Other
- **`homeLayout.ts`**: header content height **80** to match **`h-20`**.
- **`tsc --noEmit`** and **`npm test`** both pass.

**Note:** Search, movie list, and detail still use **`ContentCard`** with **`layoutVariant` default** (fluid width + 2:3). Only **home carousels** use the strict **160×240** Stitch poster module. I can mirror **160×240** on those screens too if you want the same everywhere.

---

Watchnow and details buttons size and text format on hero card not looks same as per the design. Also on every page header streamlist text and icon is not same as per the design. Match text font family, font size and font color with the design on all pages and make sure spacing on all pages looks same as per the design. use stitch mcp to match the design
